description: The shoe: what goes between the foot and the road. Built on the 'palo' of 'paloi' (foot), plus soft 'we': the foot's own covering, sandal to boot.
sound_symbolism: 'palo' names the foot it serves and 'we' wraps it softly. The word ends gentler than it begins, which is what a good shoe does to a road.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lopia wi palowe phelu.' (The child has two shoes.) 'miona palowe pilewa.' (The person makes shoes.)

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Made to the wearer's own last, mended until there was nothing left to mend. A pair of shoes was a relationship with a maker, not a purchase.
